display.purpose: Audience-size count and stat numbers only — the visual anchor.

headline.purpose: The app title in the header.

title.purpose: Panel/section headers, tab labels — uppercase, tracked.

body.purpose: All running text, table cells, hints. 65–75ch max.

label.purpose: Sub-labels, column heads, captions, button text — uppercase.

shadows.name: resting-panel
shadows.purpose: Soft ambient lift on main content panels. Calm, not dramatic.

shadows.name: floating-popover
shadows.purpose: Deeper shadow for export/save/ads popovers that overlap content.

shadows.name: toggle-selected
shadows.purpose: Tiny lift on the active segment of a segmented toggle.

motion.name: count-bar
motion.purpose: Stat progress-bar width transition as the audience resizes.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The single committed action per panel (e.g. Download).
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { font-family: 'Open Sans', sans-serif; font-size: 12px; font-weight: 700; letter-spacing: 0.04em; text-transform: uppercase; padding: 8px 16px; border-radius: 9px; border: none; background: #003057; color: #fff; cursor: pointer; transition: background 120ms ease; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: #0057B8; } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #009FDF; outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-btn-primary:disabled { opacity: .6; cursor: default; }

components.name: Ghost Button
components.description: The default action button — most actions are ghosts.
components.css: .ds-btn-ghost { font-family: 'Open Sans', sans-serif; font-size: 12px; font-weight: 700; letter-spacing: 0.04em; text-transform: uppercase; padding: 7px 14px; border-radius: 10px; border: 1px solid #D6DCE3; background: #fff; color: #3C4755; cursor: pointer; transition: border-color 120ms ease, color 120ms ease; } .ds-btn-ghost:hover { border-color: #009FDF; color: #003057; } .ds-btn-ghost:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #009FDF; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Filter Chip
components.description: Multi-select filter chip; navy fill when selected.
components.css: .ds-chip { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 6px; font-family: 'Open Sans', sans-serif; font-size: 12px; font-weight: 600; padding: 6px 12px; border-radius: 999px; border: 1px solid #D6DCE3; background: #fff; color: #3C4755; cursor: pointer; transition: all 120ms ease; } .ds-chip:hover { filter: brightness(.97); } .ds-chip-cnt { font-size: 10.5px; font-weight: 700; opacity: .62; } .ds-chip-on { border-color: #003057; background: #003057; color: #fff; } .ds-chip-on .ds-chip-cnt { opacity: .7; } .ds-chip:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #009FDF; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Search Input
components.description: Text/search field with a cyan focus border.
components.css: .ds-input { width: 100%; font-family: 'Open Sans', sans-serif; font-size: 13px; padding: 8px 10px; border: 1px solid #D6DCE3; border-radius: 10px; color: #003057; background: #fff; } .ds-input::placeholder { color: #6B7480; } .ds-input:focus { outline: none; border-color: #009FDF; }

components.name: Stat Tile
components.description: Flat white tile; the audience-count tile is the page anchor.
components.html:
12,480
Audience size
components.css: .ds-stat { position: relative; overflow: hidden; background: #fff; border: 1px solid #E4E9EF; border-radius: 16px; padding: 18px 20px; } .ds-stat-num { font-family: 'Work Sans', sans-serif; font-weight: 800; font-size: 34px; line-height: 1; color: #003057; } .ds-stat-lbl { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 7px; margin-top: 10px; font-family: 'Open Sans', sans-serif; font-size: 11px; font-weight: 700; letter-spacing: 0.09em; text-transform: uppercase; color: #6B7480; } .ds-dot { width: 6px; height: 6px; border-radius: 50%; background: #84BD00; } .ds-stat-bar { position: absolute; left: 0; bottom: 0; height: 4px; width: 64%; background: #009FDF; transition: width 400ms ease; }

components.name: Underline Tab
components.description: Underline tabs; active gets a cyan bottom border.
components.css: .ds-tabs { display: flex; gap: 4px; border-bottom: 1px solid #E4E9EF; padding: 0 20px; } .ds-tab { background: none; border: none; padding: 14px 14px 12px; font-family: 'Open Sans', sans-serif; font-weight: 700; font-size: 12px; letter-spacing: 0.06em; text-transform: uppercase; cursor: pointer; border-bottom: 3px solid transparent; margin-bottom: -1px; color: #6B7480; } .ds-tab:hover { color: #003057; } .ds-tab-on { color: #003057; border-bottom-color: #009FDF; } .ds-tab:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #009FDF; outline-offset: -4px; }

components.name: Segmented Toggle
components.description: Include/exclude or tier switch; selected segment lifts on white.
components.css: .ds-seg-toggle { display: flex; gap: 4px; padding: 3px; background: #ECEFF2; border-radius: 11px; } .ds-seg-btn { flex: 1; padding: 8px 6px; border: none; background: transparent; border-radius: 8px; font-family: 'Open Sans', sans-serif; font-weight: 700; font-size: 12px; letter-spacing: 0.04em; text-transform: uppercase; color: #6B7480; cursor: pointer; transition: all 120ms ease; } .ds-seg-on { background: #fff; color: #003057; box-shadow: 0 1px 2px rgba(15,23,33,.12); }

narrative.overview: The Eco customer base is a population of ~325,000 people, and this tool is the guide to it. A field guide is approachable and legible by design: a non-expert opens it and immediately knows how to read an entry, narrow to a region, and find what they're after. The marketer is not writing a query against a warehouse; they are leafing through a well-organized reference, watching the population resize as they refine. The generated SQL is the appendix at the back. The system is refined and restrained: paper-white surfaces, hairline structure, and the Eco brand color as deliberate punctuation — a selected chip, a focus ring, a live count — never decoration. It rejects two things: the scary SQL/DBA console and the toy consumer app.
keyCharacteristics: Paper-white canvas, hairline structure, brand color as punctuation.
The live audience count is the visual anchor of every screen.
Plain marketing language up front; SQL tucked one tab away.
Refined over decorative — restraint is the house style.
Holds up under a demo: clean hierarchy, unmistakable Eco identity.

rules.name: The Punctuation Rule
rules.body: Brand color is punctuation, not paint. On any screen the saturated brand hues should read as a handful of deliberate marks against paper-white, never as large color fields.

rules.name: The One Dark Surface Rule
rules.body: Ink-Well (#0C1A28) appears in exactly one place: the SQL code block. Dark chrome anywhere else reads as 'scary console' and is forbidden.

rules.name: The Contrast Floor
rules.body: Slate (#6B7480) on Paper/White sits near the AA edge; use it only for genuinely secondary small text. Step up to Graphite or Navy for anything decision-critical.

rules.name: The Number-Leads Rule
rules.body: Work Sans 800 at 34px is reserved for counts. Nothing else gets that weight or scale. The number always wins the hierarchy contest.

rules.name: The Uppercase-Label Ceiling
rules.body: Uppercase + wide tracking is for labels and section furniture only — never body copy, hints, or sentence-length text.

rules.name: The Flat-By-Default Rule
rules.body: Stat tiles, inputs, chips, table rows, and segments are flat at rest. Shadow is earned by floating above content, not handed out for emphasis.

dos: Do make the audience-size count the largest, most prominent element on any screen (Work Sans 800, 34px, Harbor Navy).
Do use Harbor Navy for selected/committed states and Bright Cyan for focus and hover, consistently.
Do keep brand color to deliberate punctuation against paper-white.
Do default to ghost buttons; allow at most one navy primary action per panel.
Do keep tables flat and quiet: hairline dividers, Paper hover, tabular-nums.
Do step muted text up to Graphite or Navy whenever a user must read it to make a decision.
Do preserve the cyan focus border on inputs and add visible focus on every interactive control.
donts: Don't make this look like a scary SQL/DBA console: no dark IDE chrome on the primary surface, no query editor as the landing view, no warehouse jargon in the controls.
Don't make it feel like a toy/consumer app: no oversized rounding, no playful color splashes, no emoji used as UI.
Don't drift toward a generic gradient-card SaaS dashboard — the only gradient is the 6px brand strip under the header.
Don't spread saturated brand color across large surfaces.
Don't add a second dark surface beyond the SQL block.
Don't push muted body text lighter than Slate for 'elegance'.
Don't extend uppercase + wide tracking to body copy or sentence-length text.
Don't give resting cards a drop shadow heavier than the panel ambient.
